contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Oracle QMDJ — synthesis row + always-visible structural overlay
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/19
  
  Details:
  Two operator-feedback follow-ups to PR #18 (per-cell explanation panel).
  
  1. Synthesis row at the bottom of the cell-detail list. One additional row labeled 'Reading' that takes the palace life-domain x the cell's heuristic outlook tint and renders it as a permissive verb:
     - Strong window  -> Best for <domain>
     - Favorable      -> Good for <domain>
     - Mixed          -> Workable for <domain>
     - Caution        -> Approach with caution for <domain>
     - Avoid          -> Avoid for <domain>
     Center palace overrides to 'Hold the axis' since it's classically held neutral.
  
  Domains are short concrete phrases per palace (e.g. 巽 -> wealth action, persuasion, travel; 乾 -> leadership asks, approaching authorities). Verb is sourced strictly from rules already encoded on the page, so the synthesis line can never contradict the cell tint. Dashed-accent top border visually separates it as the summary line, not another symbol row.
  
  Framing kept soft: 'Best for...' not 'Do X' — matches the existing 'heuristic synthesis, not authoritative' disclaimer.
  
  2. Unwrap the QMDJ section from its <details> collapsible. The structural overlay is part of the integrated day's read alongside the I Ching reading above — hiding it behind an 'Extended reading · QiMen Dunjia (奇門遁甲)' click-to-expand framed it as optional reference material. Replaced the <details> wrapper with a static .qmdj-section-header heading. The inner glossary 'What does each part of the chart mean?' stays collapsible — that's genuinely optional curriculum reference.
  
  No JS references to #qmdjDetails existed before unwrapping, so zero JS side effects.
  
  PR: TrueSightDAO/oracle#19 (squash-merged 06b3470).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
